mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 9.0% and continues to increase, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 9.0% and continues to increase over 2 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth outpaces housing starts growth indicates supply-side bottlenecks are easing faster than demand recovery, which historically compresses profit margins and depresses equity valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative correlation with the INDPRO/HOUST ratio spread over a 12-month rolling window (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, aggressive monetary tightening raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, but this negative effect is isolated to periods where industrial production remains below trend, confirming that rate hikes are cooling demand rather than triggering supply constraints.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not show a statistically significant negative relationship with the joint FEDFUNDS/INDPRO signal over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC > 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in residential housing starts reflects rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which historically leads positive S&P 500 forward returns through wealth effects and downstream economic multiplier impacts.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rising HOUST periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 200,000 per month for three consecutive months, it signals a labor market that is overheating, which historically precedes Fed tightening cycles and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns due to expected discount rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 200,000 for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous surge in consumer credit balances and unemployment rate indicates households are borrowing to cover living expenses rather than investing in growth, which historically signals a demand-driven earnings recession and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative correlation with the RSAFS/UNRATE divergence spread over a 12-month rolling window (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a supply-constrained recovery, a sharp rise in industrial production coupled with flat consumer credit growth indicates that corporate earnings are being driven by inventory restocking and capacity utilization rather than sustainable demand, which typically leads to mean-reverting negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.5% month-over-month while RSAFS growth falls below 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not show a statistically significant negative relationship with the INDPRO/RSAFS signal over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC > 0.02).
